metadata.description: Consolidated symptoms analysis for multiple diseases

symptoms: Sudden trouble speaking and understanding what others are saying
Paralysis or numbness of the face, arm or leg on one side of the body
Problems seeing in one or both eyes
causes: Blocked artery cutting off blood to an area of the brain (ischemic stroke)
Blood vessel leaking or bursting (hemorrhagic stroke)
prevention: Controlling high blood pressure and cholesterol
analysis.summary: A stroke occurs when a blocked artery cuts off blood to an area of the brain (ischemic stroke) or a blood vessel leaks or bursts (hemorrhagic stroke). Prompt treatment can reduce brain damage and the likelihood of death or disability. Risk factors include age, ethnicity, family history, lifestyle habits, and certain medical conditions.

symptoms: Presence of ketones in the urine.
Feeling irritable or having other mood changes.
Getting a lot of infections, such as gum, skin and vaginal infections.
causes: A group of diseases that affect how the body uses blood sugar (glucose).
The main cause of diabetes varies by type.
Prediabetes happens when blood sugar levels are higher than normal.
prevention: Taking steps to prevent prediabetes and gestational diabetes.
Getting regular check-ups with your healthcare provider.
analysis.summary: Diabetes mellitus refers to a group of diseases that affect how the body uses blood sugar (glucose). It can lead to excess sugar in the blood, which can cause serious health problems if left untreated.

symptoms: Chest pain, chest tightness, chest pressure and chest discomfort (angina)
Pain in the neck, jaw, throat, upper belly or back
Pain, numbness, weakness or coldness in the legs or arms if the blood vessels in those body areas are narrowed
causes: Buildup of fats, cholesterol and other substances in and on the artery walls (plaque)
Atherosclerosis (ath-ur-o-skluh-ROE-sis) - a buildup of plaque in the arteries
prevention: Lifestyle changes to lower risk (e.g. exercise, diet)
Managing risk factors (e.g. high blood pressure, high cholesterol)
analysis.summary: Heart disease describes a range of conditions that affect the heart, including coronary artery disease, irregular heartbeats, and congenital heart defects.

symptoms: Trouble sleeping caused by shortness of breath, coughing or wheezing
Coughing or wheezing attacks that are worsened by a respiratory virus
causes: Inflammation and swelling of the airways in the lungs
Excess mucus secretion in the airway linings
prevention: Avoiding triggers such as dust, mold, and pet dander
Using a peak flow meter to monitor lung function
Taking medication as prescribed by your doctor
Staying physically active and managing stress
Getting regular check-ups with your doctor
analysis.summary: Asthma is a condition in which the airways narrow and swell, producing extra mucus that makes breathing difficult. Symptoms can vary from person to person and may include shortness of breath, chest tightness, wheezing, and coughing. Asthma cannot be cured but its symptoms can be controlled with medication and lifestyle changes.
